verdict: 2026-07-23 07:11 PDT — The plan-stage architecture is otherwise sound, and the settled engine deferrals remain closed. It is not quite ready to ship: L1 eligibility is classified at group granularity but priced and scheduled at unit granularity, producing materially false coverage and cost in mixed groups.

findings.title: Mixed groups schedule non-code files as L1 critic units
findings.locus: .claude/skills/deep-audit/SKILL.md — Step 2 unit-map classification and Step 5 unit resolution; smoke artifact `(root)` group
findings.claim: A group becomes a code group when it contains any detected-code file, after which every file/chunk in that group enters each L1 row. The smoke artifact demonstrates the failure: the `(root)` group is code because of `install.sh`, so hidden-failure and test-adequacy runs also schedule `.gitignore` and seven Markdown units. The compiler therefore prices and claims L1 coverage for units its own non-code rule would exclude if they lived in a separate directory.
findings.alternative: Classify reviewability per unit before aggregation. Store a declaratively filtered `codeUnitIds` or lens-specific eligible-unit list for each group, and resolve L1 rows only from that list; retain the complete file list separately for inventory, coverage reporting, and any L2/L3 use.
findings.win: In the smoke plan, this removes 8 irrelevant root units from two L1 rows: 24 critic runs and 1.44M estimated tokens, reducing the stated wall-clock estimate from 30 to 21 minutes under the plan's own assumptions. It also centralizes the invariant that every scheduled L1 unit is eligible for its lens.
